creator: john@sanren.ac.za
text:
I do not think it is arm specific. I saw the same on an amd64 running 14.0BETA5 using a GENERIC kernel with INVARIANTS and WITNESS compiled in. The machine was idle when it happend, but the network is IPv6 enabled:

lock order reversal:
 1st 0xfffffe001de59a70 tcphash (tcphash, sleep mutex) @ netinet/tcp_usrreq.c:1511
 2nd 0xffffffff81aa9bc0 in6_ifaddr_lock (in6_ifaddr_lock, rm) @ netinet6/in6_src.c:303
lock order tcphash -> in6_ifaddr_lock attempted at:
#0 0xffffffff80bbeb7e at witness_checkorder+0xbbe
#1 0xffffffff80b44f89 at _rm_rlock_debug+0x139
#2 0xffffffff80d7ab2a at in6_selectsrc+0x45a
#3 0xffffffff80d7a681 at in6_selectsrc_socket+0x41
#4 0xffffffff80d785d7 at in6_pcbconnect+0x247
#5 0xffffffff80d5c7d4 at tcp6_connect+0xa4
#6 0xffffffff80d5a0eb at tcp6_usr_connect+0x2eb
#7 0xffffffff80bfc1d3 at soconnectat+0xb3
#8 0xffffffff80c03380 at kern_connectat+0xe0
#9 0xffffffff80c0328b at sys_connect+0x9b
#10 0xffffffff810492b8 at amd64_syscall+0x138
#11 0xffffffff8101aebb at fast_syscall_common+0xf8
KDB: enter: witness_checkorder
[ thread pid 2856 tid 100366 ]
Stopped at      kdb_enter+0x32: movq    $0,0xe2d1d3(%rip)

GENERIC kernel with the recent patch (https://bugs.freebsd.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=274074) to fix alignment of 32 MSI interrupts and these additions to the config:

options         DDB
options         GDB
options         BREAK_TO_DEBUGGER
options         INVARIANTS
options         INVARIANT_SUPPORT
options         WITNESS
options         WITNESS_SKIPSPIN
options         WITNESS_KDB
